differential_diagnoses: Portal vein thrombosis (cavernous transformation) due to previous abdominal trauma
next_steps: CT angiography of the mesenteric arteries to evaluate for chronic mesenteric ischemia
Measurement of serum lactate during a symptomatic postprandial episode
Liver biopsy to distinguish between cirrhosis and portal-based fibrosis (e.g., schistosomiasis)
Echocardiogram to rule out cardiac causes of posthepatic portal hypertension
Consultation with vascular surgery regarding the mesenteric vasculature
Nutritional assessment for significant weight loss
